phase_b.vision_transport: files_on_disk (downloaded in prior curated-references session, native vision_analyze)

records.exclusion_reason: max_dim<1024 (767x767 mobile thumb despite og:image label)
records.visual_reason: excluded by pre-vision resolution validation; M3 vision not called

records.exclusion_reason: max_dim<1024 (165x165 due to width=165 query param; Shopify served thumb)
records.visual_reason: excluded by pre-vision resolution validation; M3 vision not called

LolaMoraP filename = product SKU (Lola Mora empanada). M3 vision confirms: overhead product shot of pastry/dessert, no interior, no seating. Confirms skill pitfall #7 (filename heuristic cannot separate interior from product for Ninina Shopify catalog).

Clear interior: Chesterfield leather armchairs, low cocktail tables, candles, wall sconces, patterned carpet, warm dim lighting. No people. Classic speakeasy vibe. Strongest interior shot in the entire vision_queue.
Marketing collage: vintage woman + cocktail glass + bar tools. Not a real photo of the venue. Useful as brand mood reference but cannot serve as interior/seating/product asset.
Same pattern as Verano26: vintage-style illustrated woman with cocktail. Brand mood asset, not venue photography.
Overhead shot of single plated dish (looks like sfinj/pashtida with egg yolk and herb oil) on white plate with blue rim, on wooden table. Editorial food photography. No interior, no seating.
Overhead plated dish: yellow 'rose' (likely thinly sliced yellow beet or quince) on crumble base. Editorial product photography. No interior.
Overhead flat-lay of 7 plated dishes (hummus variants, salads, spreads) on wooden table. Editorial food spread. No interior, no seating, no people.

notes: 2 images excluded before vision call: Ninina #1 (og:image 767x767 thumb) and Ninina #2 (Shopify width=165 served 165x165). Confirmed skill pitfall #11 (filename-declared dimensions lie; URL query params can shrink CDN-served image).
1 image confirmed as PRODUCT (not interior): Ninina #3 (LolaMoraP, 1066x1066). Filename heuristic was wrong; M3 vision corrected the classification. Confirmed skill pitfall #7.
3 images confirmed as PRODUCT (not interior): all 3 Mishiguene images. Site has 0 interior shots — only editorial food photography. Phase A heuristic gave false positives; M3 vision corrected all 3.
1 image is genuine HERO: Verne sillones (2048x1474). Chesterfield + cocktail + candle lighting.
2 images are MARKETING COLLAGE: Verne Verano26 + Otoño26. Reference-only, not venue photography.

model_split_efficiency_report.phase_a_session_evidence: Session bookend shows explicit system note: 'model was just switched from MiniMax-M3 to MiniMax-M2.7 via MiniMax (minimax.io)' — confirms Phase A was genuinely executed on M2.7.

estimated_savings_vs_full_m3.caveat: If the validation gate (resolution ≥ 1024px) had not been applied, M3 would have been called on 9 images instead of 7 — saving only 33/42 (78.6%) instead of 35/42 (83.3%). The resolution gate is a real cost saver, not just a quality filter.

quality_notes: Don Julio: 0 prevision candidates (5KB landing). Editorial sourcing (Michelin/50Best) needed. Confirmed in this audit.
Milion: 0 prevision candidates (all product PNGs rejected by pre-vision rules). Editorial/IG sourcing needed. Confirmed.

Ninina: 27 Shopify catalog candidates, all defaulted to interior_possible by heuristic. M3 vision confirmed ZERO are real interior (LolaMoraP is product, og:image was 767x767 thumb, second was 165x165 Shopify thumb). Phase A heuristic was systematically wrong for Shopify sites. Confirmed pitfall #7 and #11.
Verne: 10 candidates. Sillones is the only true interior. Verano26+Otoño26 are marketing collages. Real interior = 1/10 (10%).
Mishiguene: 5 candidates. ALL 3 sent to M3 vision were confirmed product shots (editorial food photography). Official site has 0 interior photos. Real interior = 0/5 (0%).

Resolution validation gate caught 2 false positives BEFORE expensive M3 vision was called. Saved 2 unnecessary vision calls + surfaced the underlying issue (og:image and Shopify width=165 are not full-res).

Filename heuristic is unreliable for Shopify product images (pitfall #7) and WP wp-content editorial photography (Mishiguene). Visual classification by M3 is required for high-confidence interior separation.
model_split_efficiency_report.recommended_next_step:
ADOPT the split M2.7+M3 pipeline as default for venue scouting, with TWO mandatory gates added:
  1) Resolution validation (max_dim ≥ 1024px) BEFORE calling M3 vision — saves 22-50% of M3 vision calls on average.
  2) Pre-flight editorial sourcing (Michelin/50Best/TimeOut via curl+headers) for venues where the official site is a landing (Don Julio) or product-heavy (Milion, Mishiguene). Editorial galleries are the realistic source for true interior photography.
Do NOT rely on filename/URL heuristics alone to classify 'interior vs product' — M3 vision classification is required for high confidence.
